apex_basket.thesis: Euronet's ATM/epay/money-transfer engine trades ~9.5x TTM FCF against a CRO SoP of $150 with all five models positive, rising ROIC and expanding margins — the cheapest high-quality payments name in the book even after halving for the secular take-rate drift.
apex_basket.growth_durability: durable (+8% 3y rev CAGR, rising returns_trend, expanding gross margin, latest-Q EPS flat)

wheel.rationale: Net-debt-light FCF machine with a hard 10x-FCF floor near $50 — selling the cash-secured put pays you to wait for the multi-method MoS to re-rate.

apex_basket.skeptic_kill_fact: No fact zeroes the value math, but the SINGLE load-bearing unlock is dead-on-arrival: the May 20 2026 Investor Day -- the supposed catalyst -- was a strategy REAFFIRMATION (re-cast as a payments-infrastructure platform; 2026 targets ~$4.5B revenue / ~$800M EBITDA; reiterated 10-15% adjusted-EPS growth), NOT a sale, spin, or strategic review. The board DEFLECTED Voss Capital's strategic-alternatives demand; Voss is a NON-BINDING 4.2% holder (~1.6M shares) with no board seats and no proxy slate. The base/bull SoP (~$150/$206) only crystallizes via a transaction the board is actively resisting, with no hard forcing date -- so it is optionality the market is correctly discounting, not a triggered mispricing. The bear earnings-quality case survives fully verified: Q1'26 GAAP diluted EPS $0.83 vs $0.85 PY (-2%); FY2025 FCF $434M -29.5% YoY (TTM window FCF ~$282M < net income); Money Transfer (42% of revenue) -4% constant-currency with segment op income DOWN, driven by structural US immigration policy + a 1% remittance excise tax that management keeps calling 'transitory' across five straight calls.
apex_basket.skeptic_corrections: (1) CORRECTING THE PRIOR SKEPTIC PASS (it was wrong): the prior _skeptic/EEFT.json claimed the debate divided equity by a 'phantom ~109M' share count and capped at 2 on that basis. FALSE -- the debate JSON plainly divides by ~45.3M ($9.33B/$206, $7.27B/$160, $5.20B/$115 all => ~45.3M), which is in line with the REAL diluted weighted-average ~47.0M (Q1'26 10-Q) vs basic outstanding ~38.08M (stockanalysis 2026-06-18; 37.97M on the 2026 proxy cover). So the per-share SoP machinery is NOT broken by a share-count error, and the prior pass's central kill_fact does not hold; cap restored to 3, not 2. (2) MULTIPLE: the headline '6.5-7.3x' is on FORWARD consensus EPS (which itself bakes in the 10-15% growth guide); on TTM diluted EPS ~$6.82 the stock is ~9.8x (gurufocus/zacks, 2026-06-15), and the Graham/multi-method MoS is overstated relative to a trailing read -- genuinely cheap, but not 6.5x cheap. (3) SEGMENT EV: base-case gross segment EV ~$7.84B is ~2.5x the real ~$3.0B enterprise value (mkt cap $2.51B + net debt ~$0.5B); the 1.6-2.5x-revenue segment multiples imply ~12-14x EBITDA vs a cross-border/ATM cohort at ~5-7x (WU ~5.2x, NCR Atleos ~7.1x EV/EBITDA) -- the 'unlock' is multiple selection, not a measured discount, exactly as the peer-comps note concedes (shared-sector beta, not idiosyncratic alpha). (4) NET DEBT ~$500M as of Q1'26 (total debt ~$2.6B - cash+ATM cash ~$2.1B) -- confirmed NET DEBT, not net cash; dossier's $570M is directionally correct, slightly stale; leverage moderate (~1x EBITDA), not fortress. (5) FCF FLOOR arithmetic: with the correct ~38M share count, 10x TTM FCF ($282M) - net debt ($0.5B) = ~$59/share (not the debate's ~$50); against a ~$66 price the hard FCF floor sits ~10% below, not ~24% -- the downside cushion the thesis leans on is thinner than stated.

apex_basket.thesis: EPAM is net-cash, re-accelerating (+15% revenue YoY) and trades ~7x FCF with a CRO SoP of $120 whose bear case ($98) sits above spot; the labor-arbitrage tail is real (margin/ROIC eroding, moat_erosion CAP) so I half-size it as a deep-discount option, not a full anchor.

apex_basket.skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found. The bear's central forensic claim — that EPS is on a structurally shrinking path ('EPS FELL 8.15(2021)->6.72(2025), 5-yr CAGR -4.7%', a melting earnings base AI deflates further) — is STALE-ANCHORED and refuted by the company's own current guidance: FY2026 GAAP diluted EPS is guided to $8.29-$8.59 (per the Q1-2026 release), i.e. back ABOVE the 2021 $8.15 peak, with Q1-2026 GAAP EPS +18.8% YoY ($1.52 vs $1.28). Earnings are inflecting up, not melting down. The only durable bear point — cohort-beta cheapness with no hard idiosyncratic catalyst plus a macro-driven 2026 guide cut — is real and caps conviction, but does not break the value floor.
apex_basket.skeptic_corrections: Three load-bearing balance-sheet numbers in the debate were STALE and wrong in the value case's DISFAVOR (correcting them strengthens, not weakens, the floor): (1) Net cash is ~$872M, not $749M — the Q1-2026 10-Q shows cash $1,036.96M vs total debt only $165M (debt was overstated at $288M; net cash ~$15.50/sh, not $13.32). (2) Share count is ~54.2M weighted diluted (FY2026 guide assumes ~52.7M as the ASR shrinks the count), NOT the 56.2M used in every SoP per-share calc — fewer shares => higher per-share value. (3) FY2025 free cash flow was $612.7M (+16.3% YoY), higher than the $544M TTM figure both sides cited; Q1-2026 operating cash flow was -$36.4M, but that is EPAM's normal seasonal Q1 bonus-payout outflow (same negative Q1 in prior years), not a deterioration. CONFIRMED bear point with no correction needed: the FY2026 revenue-growth guide WAS cut to 4.0-6.5% (organic CC 2.5-5%) on macro/energy-price/North-America discretionary caution — a cyclical air-pocket, not the terminal AI-deflation impairment the bear thesis asserts.

apex_basket.thesis: H&R Block is a 25%-ROIC, 88-moat-score franchise at ~8x FCF with a CRO SoP of $50 vs $34 and a ~4.6% dividend funding the wait; DIY/AI-free-file is the secular threat but the in-office mass-market base is sticky, so it is value not trap.
apex_basket.growth_durability: durable-stable (low single-digit growth, high/stable ROIC, rising net margin, heavy buyback)

apex_basket.skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact survives independent primary-source checking. A prior skeptic pass wrote REFUTED on the premise 'HRB trades ABOVE fair value $30.70 (price $39.35), so there is no discount' -- but that is a stale/garbled anchor on BOTH legs: (a) the live quote is $34.38 (FMP, 2026-06-18; 52wk low $28.16), not $39.35; (b) $30.70 is only the EPV/Buffett FLOOR (the most conservative of several methods), NOT the screener's fair value -- the base-case SoP is $50 (range $46-54). At $34.38 vs a $50 base case a genuine ~30% discount DOES exist, so the 'no discount to harvest' kill is unsupported. The verifiable bear facts (declining absolute FCF, net debt $1.14B, negative book equity, zero open-market insider buying, spent catalyst) are all REAL and confirmed against the 10-Q/10-K, but they describe a leveraged, no-growth, buyback-driven compounder -- not a broken business: ROIC ~25% and gross margin ~44.5% are intact and assisted share inflected. The value thesis is cheap-and-real; it survives the bear attack. Hence CONFIRMED, with corrections, not REFUTED.
apex_basket.skeptic_corrections: (1) FCF OVERSTATED in the bull/dossier: the load-bearing 'TTM FCF ~$761M' exceeds EVERY actual annual FCF since FY23 (FY25 $598.8M, FY24 $657.2M, FY23 $752.1M per 10-K cash-flow statements). Honest normalized free-cash base is ~$600M, not $761M; at ~$600M/~128M sh that is ~$4.70 equity-FCF (vs $5.95 used), so the FCF cross-check leg of fair value is ~15-20% thinner than presented and the bear's 'buyback flatters per-share, absolute cash is flat-to-down (-8.9% YoY, confirmed exactly)' critique is STRONGER than the bull allowed. (2) GAAP EPS FLATTERED: Q3-FY26 GAAP EPS includes an $84.1M one-time non-cash IRS-examination tax benefit (+~$0.65); the sop_bull correctly strips this from the $5.15 normalized adj-EPS, so the valuation is not impaired, but any read off the GAAP $6.61 would be. (3) OMITTED REGULATORY BRAKE: the debate missed the FTC final order (finalized Jan-2025, $7M) governing HRB's 2025 AND 2026 tax-season conduct -- mandated 'free'-filing advertising disclosures, a ban on deleting consumer data on downgrade, plus a Meta/Google tracking-pixel data-sharing allegation; immaterial to cash but a real ongoing constraint on the DIY downgrade/'free' funnel the bull already concedes is ceded. (4) PRICE DRIFT: spot $34.38 sits BELOW the debate's $36 -- the FIRED catalyst has NOT produced convergence, validating the bear's 'second unproven leg / re-rate window spent' point. Net debt $1,139.0M (10-Q) verifies the regime file's $1.14B exactly; the prior pass's $1.60B bear figure and its $0.62-0.94B 'correction' are both wrong.

apex_basket.thesis: Universal Health's acute + behavioral mix compounds at 5-6% same-store with margins expanding and a CRO SoP of $210 vs $141; balance sheet is deleveraging with no maturity wall, but the DPP-2028 regulatory rent is shared sector beta, so I cap it against THC.
apex_basket.growth_durability: durable (+9.7% revenue YoY, expanding acute margin, low/falling leverage) but ~$1.2B EBITDA is DPP regulatory rent compressing from 2028
apex_basket.skeptic_corrections: (1) NET DEBT is ~$4.59B, NOT $5.01B. Verified against the primary Q1-26 10-Q (uhs-20260331) balance sheet: current maturities of LT debt $756.24M + LT debt $3,952.118M = total debt $4,708.358M, less cash $119.028M = net debt $4.589B. FY25 release agrees (total debt $4.753B, cash $137.8M). The debate's $5.01B net debt is overstated by ~$0.4B; a prior skeptic pass that asserted '$5.01B exact / total debt $5,125,817K' is also wrong against this primary filing. Correcting net debt DOWN raises the SoP per-share (helps the bull). (2) SHARE COUNT is 61.67M diluted (Q1-26 10-Q), vs the 64.5M the debate's SoP divides by — again raising per-share value. Neither correction breaks the bear; both modestly aid value, so the floor holds. (3) NEW POST-DEBATE FACT that DEEPENS the bear's structural tail and was NOT haircut in the $210 FV: CMS-2449-P (NPRM, Federal Register 2026-05-22) goes BEYOND OBBBA — it caps ALL state-directed payments at 100%/110% of Medicare (not just OBBBA's four specified SDPs), cuts current-law SDP dollar amounts 10%/yr to the Medicare limit within ~10 years, and CMS scores ~$775B of decade Medicaid reductions; AHA/FAH flagged member financial-stability risk; comments close 2026-07-21. The exact valley-of-death trigger the debate treated as hypothetical (CMS rulemaking pricing the cut worse/broader/sooner than the $360-400M-by-2032 base, which the CEO already labels 'worst case') has now materialized as a live rule. (4) CASH-LAG CONFIRMED vs primary: FY25 OCF $1.864B less capex $1.015B = ~$849M FCF vs $1.489B GAAP net income, with a +$145M increase in Medicaid-supplemental net receivables booked in 2025 — DPP-receivable timing flatters the P&L, exactly as the bear claims; ROIC ~9% near WACC and the discount is shared sector beta, not idiosyncratic alpha. Stale, non-load-bearing note: the $122M DOJ False Claims Act settlement (behavioral-segment medically-unnecessary admissions + kickbacks) is from 2020-07-10 with a now-expired 5-yr corporate integrity agreement — resolved, not a live disqualifier.

wheel.rationale: Net-cash WIDE-moat compounder with a bear SoP only ~7% below spot — selling the put is paid to own a quality franchise at a discount while the China discount unwinds.

apex_basket.skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found. I attacked the bear case to break the value thesis and every load-bearing fact either verified against primary filings or was conservative in the bull's favor. The thesis survives. The bear's strongest structural brake — the China-ADR / HFCAA / VIE overhang — is real but NOT disqualifying: the FY2025 Form 20-F discloses VIEs generated 85.2% of net revenue and ADSs could be barred under HFCAA if PCAOB access lapsed, but PCAOB has held complete inspection access to mainland-China/HK auditors since Dec-2022 (the delisting timer is vacated, not running), so this is a dormant political re-escalation tail and the reason the multiple is permanently discounted — not an active disqualifier. The cheapness (P/FCF ~11.6x on a net-cash, ~30%-net-margin, ~20% EPS-CAGR franchise) is real and primary-source-verified.
skeptic_corrections: FORCING FUNCTION EXISTS — bear/moderator claim FALSE: the debate's central knock ('capital allocation under-narrated; company conspicuously silent on buyback; NO dated forcing function') is refuted by the filings. NetEase runs a US$5.0B repurchase program EXTENDED on 2025-11-20 to run through Jan-9-2029, with ~US$2.1B / 23.2M ADSs deployed by Q1-2026 (~42%, ~$2.9B runway remaining), PLUS a recurring quarterly dividend (US$1.16/ADS FY2025; US$0.72/ADS declared Q1-2026). This is an active, dated, multi-year capital-return forcing function the debate said was missing.

NET CASH UNDERSTATED, not overstated: debate used RMB159.95B. Primary releases report net cash RMB163.5B (US$23.4B) at Dec-31-2025 and RMB167.5B (US$24.3B) at Mar-31-2026 (cash/deposits/investments minus loans). The downside cushion / SoP add-back is LARGER than assumed. (Note: gross liquidity is ~RMB170.9B vs ~RMB10.96B debt — do not conflate the gross figure with net cash.)

40% ONSHORE-TRAPPED-CASH HAIRCUT TOO PUNITIVE: NetEase funds ~US$4B/yr of ADR-holder cash returns (buyback + dividend) from operating cash flow WITHOUT borrowing, so the cash demonstrably reaches ADR holders; the sop_bear 40% haircut overstates repatriation friction.

'+3% EPS DECELERATION CONFIRMS THE BEAR' IS STALE: Q1-2026 (reported 2026-05-21) shows revenue +6.1% YoY, GAAP net income RMB10.7B (up from RMB10.3B), net margin 34.9% (ABOVE FY25 30%), GAAP per-ADS US$2.42 vs US$2.35. Stable-to-modestly-growing, not deteriorating; the bear's margin-compression pillar is not visible in the actual quarter.
No dilution / no debt-wall disqualifier: diluted share count flat-to-shrinking (~645.6M FY25 vs ~646.1M FY24); RMB10.96B short-term debt vs RMB170.9B liquidity.

apex_basket.thesis: SCOR trades at a deep discount to book with a CRO SoP of EUR 38-40 vs EUR 31 and a ~6.2% dividend; its low Altman-Z is a reinsurance reserve/float artifact (funded solvency exempt, 128x coverage), and the life-book recovery is the value re-rate, not a catalyst.
apex_basket.growth_durability: recovering (life-book underwriting turn, rising net margin off a low base; ROIC still thin)

wheel.rationale: Price already near the bear/trust-discounted floor with a 6.2% dividend underneath — selling the put adds income while book-value convergence plays out.

apex_basket.skeptic_corrections: No kill_fact: every load-bearing value number verifies against SCOR's own Q4/FY-2025 release — FY2025 NI EUR 851m (EUR 846m adj), ROE 19.2% (19.1% adj), solvency 215% at 31-Dec-2025, economic value EUR 8.5bn / EUR 48 per share (the bull's NAV anchor), EV +13.7% at constant economics, dividend EUR 1.90 (+5.6%). The discount is EV-based (~0.65x SCOR's own disclosed economic value), a genuine NAV discount on a recapitalized Tier-1 balance sheet, NOT merely a low headline P/E — so a real (if slow) value edge survives the bear. BUT two corrections are required, one to the bear and one caveat to the bull. (A) BEAR CORRECTION (load-bearing): the bear's single largest claimed left-tail — sop_bear OVERLAY '-€2 to -€4/share un-provisioned arbitration tail' and bear_thesis Pillar 3 'a ~US$1bn US-life retro arbitration ... an adverse, un-provisioned award is the single largest left-tail' — is mis-stated on TWO counts against primary sources. (i) DIRECTIONALITY: SCOR is the CLAIMANT, having filed the 2022 arbitration 'seeking the EXECUTION of the retrocession treaties signed with Covéa in June 2021' — it is trying to ENFORCE risk-transfer it wants, and in Aug-2025 the tribunal REJECTED Covéa's bid to stay proceedings (a procedural win for SCOR). It is a genuine two-sided binary, but the bear's one-directional '-€2/-€4 adverse award' framing is not the established asymmetry. (ii) 'UN-PROVISIONED' IS FALSE: SCOR discloses 'all major arbitrations and disputes in which SCOR is involved are provisioned at best estimate in SCOR's audited financial statements.' The bear's specific un-provisioned overlay rests on a factual error and should be removed/narrowed — this WEAKENS the bear, not the bull. (B) BULL CAVEAT (why conviction is still capped at 3, one notch under the bull's 4): the bear's STRUCTURAL points survive unrefuted and are NOT a kill — sub-scale #4/#5 quality gap that justifies a permanent partial peer discount (net margin ~5.8% vs Munich Re ~11%); a shrinking top line (-5.4% YoY) into a softening cycle with the 4-6% P&C revenue target demoted to 'outcome'; sub-81% combined ratios flattered by a benign 6.8%-vs-10% nat-cat year; reserve credibility not re-earned in one year (management itself won't 'declare victory' on L&H for 2+ years; BVPS 5y CAGR -7.9%; two reserve detonations in five years — 2022 net loss, FY2024 NI ~EUR 4m); and a SOFT, two-sided catalyst set (Covéa coin-flip + agency-paced rating upgrade). The cost-of-equity re-rate is also partly being competed away exogenously (Fitch Outlook -> Positive Oct-2025), so the EV-discount harvest is slow, not event-forced.

apex_basket.thesis: Harmony Biosciences is net-cash, 18.8% ROIC and ~5.6x FCF on Wakix with a CRO SoP of $46 vs $33; the stale latest-Q (-29% EPS YoY) makes the SoP provisional and the single-drug concentration is the risk, so I keep it half-plus as the orthogonal healthcare diversifier.
apex_basket.growth_durability: growing top line (+22% revenue YoY, 18.8% ROIC) but eroding gross margin and single-product (Wakix) concentration

runner_ups.note: Gartner at ~7.8x FCF, SoP $175 vs $127 — bumped from apex only to keep ai-displacement <=2 names (WKL.AS+HRB held the budget); first swap-in if either is exited.

Pluxee SoP EUR 17.5 vs 11.43, but peer_verdict=modest_discount_sector_beta (shared Brazil-PAT/Italy-voucher regulatory de-rate vs Edenred) — discounted as sector beta, kept as a watch leg not an apex seat; re-clusters with EEFT on payments-disintermediation.
Deckers (UGG/Hoka) 88-moat, net cash, SoP $150 vs $109 — high quality but peak EPS (epr 3.65) and verdict C; runner-up behind HRB for the Consumer Cyclical seat.
Cognizant net-cash at ~8x FCF, SoP $51 vs $44 — solid but lower MoS and re-clusters with EPAM on labor-arbitrage-deflation; Tech sector already at 3.
Swiss Re WIDE-moat reinsurer, SoP ~150 vs 123 + 6.5% dividend — re-clusters with SCR.PA on the reinsurance axis, so held as the orthogonal-financials runner-up not a second reinsurer seat.
TriNet PEO at ~7x FCF, SoP $55 vs $45 — cheap vs PAYX/ADP but ERODING moat + flat revenue + labor-arbitrage tail; orthogonal-industrials reserve.
Sopra Steria SoP EUR 200 vs 142 at ~5.2x FCF, but moat_erosion CAP + labor-arbitrage-deflation cohort; reserve behind EPAM.

value_memo:
RUBRIC WEIGHTING: four pillars ~25 pts each on names that clear the gate — (1) Margin of Safety anchored on CRO sop_mos_pct cross-checked vs 5-method mos_spread agreement; (2) cyclical-peak/stale normalization (credit cheapness only on normalized earnings); (3) funded-leverage solvency (net_funded_debt/EBITDA + coverage, raw Altman-Z ignored); (4) multiples vs true peers + growth/quality durability. The catalyst-watch regime overlay is fully removed: a live catalyst is neither credit nor requirement. System of record = debate sop_mos_pct + forensic_gate + regime-blind value_conviction (preferred over regime-tilted debate_conviction throughout).

FORENSIC-GATE EXCLUSIONS (interrogator credibility <=2 => INELIGIBLE): AFX.DE (interr 2, deteriorating, sop null), BRBR (interr 2), CORT (interr 2, MoS -78% with the stock detached from fundamentals on resubmission headlines). CAP-gated (value_score <=~50, DETERIORATING-but-credible): ATE.PA, DFH, PPC, and the broader moat_erosion=CAP set — none seated above their cap.

BRBR (Premier Protein) — EXPLICIT CALL-OUT: scan_headline_mos +192% with a 5/5 raw model agreement looks like the cheapest name in the file, but forensic_gate=EXCLUDE (interr 2) and the CRO returned sop_mos_pct=null (could not stand behind a fair value) — the raw MoS is a forensic artifact, weak funded leverage (4.21x, 3.5x coverage), DETERIORATING trajectory. INELIGIBLE; the +192% is fake.

CALM (Cal-Maine Foods) — EXPLICIT CALL-OUT: the textbook cyclical-peak artifact. scan_headline_mos +378% and 5/5 models positive, but eps_peak_ratio 9.17 on the avian-flu egg windfall, freshness_stale=true, latest-Q PIVOTING, eps_normalized $19 base vs a peak FY. The CRO already normalized it: sop_mos_pct collapsed to +19% — i.e. the cheapness is ~95% peak-earnings illusion. Not seated; a low multiple on a 9x-peak egg cycle is NOT value.

CYCLICAL-PEAK / STALE NORMALIZATION APPLIED to seated names: every apex name with peak_flag=true was credited only on the CRO's already-normalized SoP (EEFT epr1.6, CMCSA epr2.0 + stale post-Versant anchor, WKL.AS epr2.2, THC epr3.3, UHS epr2.5, HRB epr1.5, NTES epr2.7) — all retained a positive normalized MoS, so the cheapness survives. Other peak artifacts down-rated and NOT seated: BYD (epr 6.2, scan +397% -> SoP +8.6%), ANF (epr 17.4 on peak retail), MAU.PA (scan +248%), AMD/AVGO/ANET/ASML (peak semis, negative normalized SoP).

FUNDED-LEVERAGE SOLVENCY: raw Altman-Z ignored. SCR.PA (Z 1.75) and SREN.SW (Z 0.55) cleared as reinsurance float/reserve artifacts (funded_solvency exempt_financial, coverage 129x / 40x). THC at 2.33x funded / 4.2x coverage is the most-levered seat — kept because the dossier confirms NO maturities until late-2027 and a 3.1x->2.24x deleveraging trajectory (one moderate metric, no wall = acceptable). UHS 1.79x with falling leverage, fine. No seated name is jointly weak (>3.5x AND <3x coverage AND a maturity wall). net_debt_exceeds_mktcap names (CMCSA, CAG, CA.PA) were never credited as net-cash.

RISE/FALL vs PRIOR VALUE APEX (2026-06-18: ADBE, PLX.PA, NTES, WKL.AS, UHS, EEFT, MMS, THC, PRDO):
  - HELD: EEFT (0.9->1.1, top MoS, upsized as anchor), WKL.AS (1.0->0.8, trimmed under the ai-displacement cap), NTES (1.1 held, the durable WIDE-moat anchor), UHS (0.9->0.6, cut into the THC/UHS combined cap), THC (0.5 held, capped vs UHS), CMCSA (RUNNER-UP -> APEX, 0.5: deep 5/5 MoS, the cheapest cable name).
  - DROPPED FROM APEX: ADBE (was 0.5; moat_erosion=CAP + ERODING moat with falling returns_trend/net margin and the #1 ai-displacement tail — relative to WKL.AS/HRB its normalized MoS is weaker and the theme budget is full; out of the slate entirely). PLX.PA (was 0.5 -> RUNNER-UP: peer_verdict downgraded to modest_discount_sector_beta, its discount-to-Edenred is shared Brazil-PAT/Italy regulatory beta, not alpha — demoted to a watch leg). MMS (was 0.5 -> dropped: re-rated, asymmetry gone, moat_erosion CAP, labor-arbitrage tail; EPAM is the better-MoS expression of that theme). PRDO (Perdoceo) — NOT IN THE 140-NAME INPUT this run, so it falls out by absence, not by demerit.
  - NEW IN: EPAM (0.5, net-cash deep-value IT-services option), HRB (0.7, 88-moat tax franchise), SCR.PA (0.7, reinsurance book-value re-rate), HRMY (0.6, orthogonal net-cash pharma).

correlation_stress: Decomposed the final 10 on hidden factors against the measured 2y-weekly matrix (avg pairwise 0.18; only pair >=0.6 is UHS-THC at 0.64).
  - [>=2] reimbursement-compression / hospital-reimbursement: THC + UHS — the highest measured pair (0.64) and a single shared real-world factor (the 2028 Medicaid-DPP / EAPTC reimbursement glide hits BOTH Hospital segments at once). RESOLUTION = kept-with-combined-cap: combined_caps THC+UHS max_units 1.1 on axis secular-theme:reimbursement-compression (THC 0.5 + UHS 0.6), so one reimbursement ruling cannot flush two full-size legs. A single DPP ruling now moves at most ~1.1 units, not ~1.7. Considered diversifying by swapping one for an orthogonal healthcare name — HRMY (single-drug, uncorrelated to hospital policy) is already seated as the orthogonal third healthcare leg, so the cohort is one capped pair + one orthogonal name rather than three reimbursement legs.
  - [>=2] secular-theme ai-displacement: WKL.AS + HRB. Both are non-eroding anchors that merely CARRY the narrative (WKL.AS rising ROIC/recurring info-software; HRB 25%-ROIC sticky tax franchise) rather than eroding labor-arbitrage shops, and their end-markets (EU legal/tax/health information vs US mass-market consumer tax prep) share almost no demand driver — real co-movement is low despite the shared tag. RESOLUTION = kept-with-combined-cap: combined_caps WKL.AS+HRB max_units 1.5 on axis secular-theme:ai-displacement (0.8 + 0.7). I explicitly did NOT seat ADBE/IT/GLOB — the live ai-displacement cohort the rubric flags — so the eroding tail of this theme is empty and the budget carries only two durable anchors.
  - [resolved to 1] payments-disintermediation: EEFT seated; PLX.PA demoted to runner-up specifically so this melting-together cohort carries ONE name, not two (EEFT take-rate vs Pluxee voucher-float would have been shared fintech-disintermediation beta).
  - [resolved to 1] labor-arbitrage-deflation: EPAM seated; MMS dropped and CTSH/SOP.PA/TNET held as runner-ups so the IT-services/BPO compression cohort carries ONE apex leg.
  - [resolved to 1] reinsurance-cycle: SCR.PA seated; SREN.SW held as runner-up (the rubric flags SREN.SW re-clusters with SCR.PA) so the book carries ONE reinsurer.
  - [advertising-cycle CMCSA+SAX.DE]: SAX.DE (Stroer OOH advertising) NOT seated (sop_mos only +9.7%, marginal), so the CMCSA/SAX.DE advertising pair the rubric flags collapses to ONE seated ad-cycle name (CMCSA). No combined cap needed.

BEAR REBUTTAL (one sentence per seat, written before final sizing):
  - EEFT: the strongest bear is that stablecoin/wallet rails arbitrage the cross-border take-rate and ATM cash usage toward zero, so today's 9.5x FCF is a melting-ice-cube multiple, not a discount (sized 1.1 anyway because all 5 models and rising margins say the erosion is slower than the price implies — break $50).
  - CMCSA: the bear is that fixed-wireless + fiber overbuild permanently re-rates broadband ARPU lower and the 3.7x multiple is correct for a structurally shrinking cash flow (half-sized at 0.5 with an $18 terminal-erosion break for exactly this reason).
  - WKL.AS: the bear is that gen-AI collapses the willingness-to-pay for syndicated legal/tax/health content and the EUR-105 SoP rests on a peak EPS that AI deflates (kept because it sells workflow-embedded software, not static content — but trimmed to 0.8).
  - EPAM: the bear is that AI decouples revenue from billable headcount and bill-rates deflate faster than the re-acceleration, validating the ERODING moat and eroding margins (so it is only a half-unit deep-discount option, not an anchor).
  - HRB: the bear is that IRS Direct File + AI free-file structurally erode the paid mass-market tax base and the 88 moat score is a legacy-inertia score, not a switching-cost one (sized 0.7 with a $30 EPV-floor break).
  - UHS: the bear is that the ~$1.2B DPP regulatory rent (a third of segment EBITDA) compresses from 2028 and the behavioral 'inflection' is an unproven forward promise, so the SoP overstates terminal earnings (capped at 0.6 inside the reimbursement pair).
  - THC: the bear is that the EAPTC expiration ($250M 2026 drag) plus the DPP glide hits the Hospital segment while peak FCF (epr 3.26, +127% YoY) mean-reverts, making 6x FCF a peak-of-cycle multiple (capped at 0.5 with a $150 trough break).
  - NTES: the bear is China ADR/policy and game-approval risk — a regulatory tail that can re-rate the whole multiple regardless of the 29%-net-cash and quality (sized 1.1 because the net-cash floor puts the bear SoP only ~7% below spot).
  - SCR.PA: the bear is that the life-book reserve adequacy and nat-cat exposure mean the 'cheap to book' is a justified discount for an under-earning reinsurer (ROIC ~0%), not a re-rate (sized 0.7, leaning on the exempt-financial solvency and 6.2% dividend).
  - HRMY: the bear is single-drug concentration — Wakix patent/generic risk plus the -29% latest-Q EPS makes the SoP provisional and the franchise a one-product bet (sized 0.6 as the orthogonal diversifier, entry on weakness).

Sizing reconciliation: every moat_erosion=CAP seat carries size_units <=0.5 (CMCSA 0.5, EPAM 0.5, THC 0.5); CRO-only / stale-anchor caveats noted in-thesis; combined-cap members (THC 0.5+UHS 0.6<=1.1; WKL.AS 0.8+HRB 0.7<=1.5) all match their combined_caps numbers above. No hidden factor quietly carries two full-size legs.

stress_test.note: Market-based stress: weighted basket return to the 52-week lows, and to 52w-lows -15% (recession). cro_bear is the agents' own adverse SoP (bear_fv_px); when missing or implying upside it is flagged invalid and the published downside is the market-based recession stress.

correlation.fx_note: EU names in local ccy; correlations unadjusted for FX.

pool_stats.banner: Best-of-B basket: 0 verdict-A names in a 140-name pool — every apex pick is a verdict-B value name. Expect SLOW gap-closure: margin-of-safety re-rating, no hard catalysts by design.

funnel.note: scan MoS = membership/divergence signal only; never a rank or weight
